purpose: Per-parser-family AST-shape contract for the SystemVerilog grammar. The generated SV parser is NOT in the default `cargo test --features generated_parsers` build; it is produced on-demand by `sv_stimuli_quality_gate` (and similar) into `rust/target/<gate>/work/systemverilog_parser.rs` and discarded. The cfg `has_generated_systemverilog_parser` therefore stays off in default builds and the matching unit test compiles out. When the parser is present (gate run or `PGEN_SYSTEMVERILOG_PARSER_PATH` override), the test activates and the contract is enforced.
doctrine: First run after the parser becomes available will probably hit a regression-lock failure because `current_content_kind` here is a placeholder calibrated from grammar inspection rather than from running the parser. The expected workflow on first activation is: read the regression-lock message, update `current_content_kind` to the observed value, and commit the manifest update alongside the parser availability change.
layout_note: [grammars/systemverilog.ebnf](../../../grammars/systemverilog.ebnf) line 195 declares `-> {type: "systemverilog_file", source_text: $2}`, but the annotation is positioned AFTER the helper rule `sv_multi_entry_root := systemverilog_file | library_text | systemverilog_parseable_file` (line 193). In typical EBNF parsing logic an annotation latches onto the most recent rule, which would make this annotation attach to `sv_multi_entry_root` rather than `systemverilog_file`. The annotation's `$2` reference is consistent with `systemverilog_file` (whose body has 3 elements where $2 is `source_text`) but inconsistent with `sv_multi_entry_root` (Or-root, $2 = `library_text`). Whether this is a positional grammar bug or a tolerated EBNF-parsing convention should be confirmed during first calibration.

samples.name: minimal_module
samples.drift_tracked_in: PGEN: SV per-family contract calibration on first parser availability + regen coordination follow-up. See layout_note above.
